---
title: "Flight Delays"
subtitle: "Unit 3: Data Cleaning, JSON, and EDA"
author: "David Martinez"
format:
  html:
    self-contained: true
    page-layout: full
    title-block-banner: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    toc-location: body
    number-sections: false
    html-math-method: katex
    code-fold: true
    code-summary: "Show the code"
    code-overflow: wrap
    code-copy: hover
    code-tools:
        source: false
        toggle: true
        caption: See code
execute: 
  warning: false
---

## Summary

This project focuses on cleaning and exploring a messy JSON dataset of airport flight delays.

### Data Wrangling

Cleaned a messy JSON dataset of airport flight delays. Standardized various missing data types (e.g., `-999`, `"n/a"`, empty strings, and `"1500+"`) into consistent `np.nan` values for accurate analysis.

### Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA)

Calculated the proportion of delays caused by weather, carriers, and security. Identified the worst airports for weather delays and determined the best month to fly to avoid delays overall.

### Visualization

Built bar charts using `lets_plot` to visualize delay proportions across different airports and months, utilizing data reshaping techniques like `pd.melt()` to compare different delay types side-by-side.
